name: Kratom
description: Kratom (Mitragyna speciosa) is a tropical evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, and Myanmar. In Southeast Asian traditions, Kratom holds significant cultural and practical importance. It has been used for centuries by indigenous populations for its medicinal and stimulant properties. Historically, Kratom leaves were chewed by farmers and laborers to combat fatigue, increase productivity, and alleviate pain during long hours of work in the fields. It was also used socially, similar to coffee or tea in other cultures, fostering community and shared experiences. In some regions, Kratom played a role in traditional medicine, employed to treat various ailments such as diarrhea, coughs, and muscle aches. While not explicitly linked to specific deities in major pantheons, Kratom is often regarded with reverence as a gift from nature, embodying the life-giving forces of the rainforest. Its use is intertwined with local customs and beliefs concerning the spiritual power of plants and their ability to enhance physical and mental well-being. Traditional preparations involve chewing fresh leaves, brewing them into tea, or drying and crushing them into powder. Kratom remains an integral part of the cultural heritage of Southeast Asia, reflecting the deep connection between people and the natural world.
subtitle: Sacred Herb of Southeast Asia

keyMyths.title: The Gift of the Forest
keyMyths.description: Legend says a benevolent forest spirit gifted Kratom to the people to ease their burdens and provide strength for their daily lives. It became a symbol of the forest's generosity.

sources.author: Suwanlert, S.
